lines.speaker: Introduction
lines.japanese: Welcome to Marugoto: Japanese Language and Culture, a comprehensive series of

lines.speaker: coursebooks
lines.japanese: for adult learners of Japanese as a foreign language developed by the Japan

lines.speaker: Foundation
lines.japanese: and based on the JF Standard for Japanese Language Education.

lines.speaker: The
lines.japanese: Japan Foundation engages in a variety of cultural exchange initiatives aimed at

lines.speaker: deepening
lines.japanese: understanding of Japan overseas and promoting mutual understanding between

lines.speaker: Japan
lines.japanese: and other countries. We think it is important that our work, including our work in

lines.speaker: Japanese
lines.japanese: language education, takes place in a way that encourages mutual understanding

lines.speaker: between
lines.japanese: people in situations where international cultural exchange takes place, and

lines.speaker: Marugoto
lines.japanese: Japanese Language and Culture is based on this way of thinking.

lines.speaker: The
lines.japanese: word Marugoto means 'whole' or 'everything', and was chosen as the title of

lines.speaker: the
lines.japanese: coursebook because the course encompasses both language and culture, features

lines.speaker: communication
lines.japanese: between people in a range of situations, and allows you to experience a

lines.speaker: variety
lines.japanese: of aspects of Japanese culture through hundreds of colourful photographs and

lines.speaker: illustrations
lines.japanese: The coursebook also incorporates many innovative components for learning language

lines.speaker: including
lines.japanese: learning divided into two volumes:Katsudoo (coursebook for communicative language

lines.speaker: language
lines.japanese: competences), aimed atimproving ability in language knowledge- so that you

lines.speaker: can
lines.japanese: choose a method of study that meets your needs and suits your learning style

lines.speaker: designed
lines.japanese: with an emphasis on understanding and respecting other cultures, and

lines.speaker: Japanese
lines.japanese: learning Japanese through listening to a variety of natural contextualized conversations

lines.speaker: management
lines.japanese: of your own learning through a portfolio approach

lines.speaker: the

exercises.question: Marugoto:Japanese Language and Culture Starter A1
Lesson 2 Would you say that again? Moo ichido onegaishimasu
Write your name and country in Japanese
tte doo yuu imi desu ka./...
Lesson 3 Nice to meet you Doozo yoroshiku
Recognise the parts of a business card
Lesson 4 There are three people in my family Kazoku wa san-nin desu
Kono hito wa dare desu ka.
Tell someone about your family. using a family photo
Lesson 5 What kind of food do you like? Nani ga suki desu ka
Lesson 6 Where are you going to have lunch today? Doko de tabemasu ka
Say what your favorite dish is
Sukina ryoori wa nan desu ka.
Talk with a friend about where to go for lunch
Kyoo wa doko de tabemasu ka.
Order food and drinks at a hamburger shop
Lesson 7 There are three rooms in my home Heya ga mittsu arimasu
Say what kind of home you live in
Watashi no ie wa apaato desu.
Say what you have in your home
Watashi no ie wa semai desu.
Write an E-mail inviting someone to your home
Watashi no ie ni eakon ga futatsu arimasu.
Lesson 8 It's a nice room li heya desu ne
Ask/Say where to put things in the room
Gomenkudasai./li heya desu ne./Kore, nan desu ka.
Recognise the name and address on signs
Lesson 9 What time do you get up? Nan-ji ni okimasu ka
Say the time you do something
Lesson 1O When is convenient for you? Itsu ga ii desu ka
Talk about your schedule for this week
Talk about when to have a party

Yasumi no hi ni nani o shimasu ka.
Yasumi no hi ni supootsu o shimasu ka.

Say whether or not you will attend an event
Talk about whether or not you can go out with your
· lssho ni mi ni ikimasen ka.

Show a taxi driver a note and say your destination
Ikura desu ka./Kaado de ii desu ka.
Say how to get to a particular destination
Koko kara kuukoo made doo yatte ikimasu ka.
Lesson 1 4 It's a famous temple Yuumeena otera desu
Say how to get to a destination, using a map
Sumimasen, depaato wa doko desu ka.

Talk about whatyou want to buy

Kawaii T-shatsu wa doko de kaemasu ka.
Lesson 16 Ill take this Kore kudasai
Make a brief comment on things in a shop
Lesson 17 It was fun Tanoshikatta desu
Say what you did on your days off
Say briefly what you thought about your days off
Write a short blog about your days off

コーヒーショップでアルバイトをしました。
まちでともだちにあいました。
いっしょにのみにいきました。
うちでそうじとせんたくをしました。
Uchi de sooji to sentaku o shimashita.
こうえんでこどもとあびました。(へへ)
あなたはやすみになにを
What did you do on your days off?
How did they feel about it?
こうえんでこどもとあそびました
はなびたいかいはどうでしたか。
だい17かたのしかつたです
あなたのまちでどんなイベントがありましたか。
What kinds of event have there been in your town recently?
せんしうのはなびたいかい
ビートルズをうたいました。
らいしゆうまたいきます。
Tsugi wa Kyooto ni ikitai desu
パウロさんはのりかさんにEメールをかきました。
Paulo-san wrote an e-mail to Norika-san.
きょう あさくさに いきました。
きょう マリアおばあちゃんと 日本に
きました。うれしいです!
そばをたべました。たいへんでした。
かいものしました!これ、かいました!
でも、おいしかったです。
マリアさんは30年ぶりに東京にいきました。
Maria-san went to Tokyo for the first time in thirty years.
Soo desu ka. Tookyoo de nani o shimashita ka.
ありがとうございます。
Tsugi wa Kyooto ni ikitai desu
Four people went on a trip to Tokyo.
(2)つぎはどこにいきたいですか。
What did they do in Tokyo?

つぎはどこにいきたいですか。
